subject: Re: Centring Tomograms in WARP Reconstruction
body:
Hi Katie,

Adjusting the reconstruction dimensions as suggested by Alister is probably
the quickest way to move forward without having to redo your alignments.
But another thing to check would be the options that you are using to align
the tilt series. If you are using AreTomo, the -AlignZ option is especially
important to get well-centered tomograms. It should roughly match the
thickness of your lamella slab, which may be different for each tomo.

Best wishes,

--
Ricardo Diogo Righetto


Em qua., 8 de jan. de 2025 às 16:31, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com>
escreveu:

> Hi Katie,
>
> Ah! Yes, you specify tomogram dimensions in your tilt series settings file
> - you can adjust these as you see fit :-)
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Jan 8, 2025, at 07:12, Katie Downes <katiegdownes@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿
> Hi Alister,
>
> When the tomogram isn’t centred sometimes information is lost at the top
> or bottom.
>
> When I’ve come across this problem in the past when reconstructing in
> AreTomo of Relion5 I usually increase the depth of the tomogram I.e. VolZ.
> Is there a way of doing something similar in warp?
>
> Best,
>
> Katie
>
> Sent from Outlook for iOS <https://aka.ms/o0ukef>
> ------------------------------
> *From:* Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com>
> *Sent:* Wednesday, January 8, 2025 2:46:27 PM
> *To:* Katie Downes <katiegdownes@gmail.com>
> *Cc:* Warp <warp-em@googlegroups.com>
> *Subject:* Re: Centring Tomograms in WARP Reconstruction
>
> Hi Katie,
>
> Happy new year! 🥳
>
> Control over tomogram centering is only implicitly exposed via the ability
> to import tilt series alignments - we don’t expose it or provide any
> utilities for this.
>
> Could you elaborate on why you would like this control?
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Jan 8, 2025, at 02:29, Katie Downes <katiegdownes@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿Hello and Happy New Year Warpers!
>
> How do you centre a tomogram in the reconstruction using warp?
>
> I have a batch of tomograms, the majority of which are centred, however
> one is too low and the other is too high. I'm not sure how to tinker with
> the reconstruction parameters in general given that the command doesn't
> have too many inputs.
>
> This is my command: WarpTools ts_reconstruct --settings
> warp_tiltseries.settings --angpix 10 --halfmap_frames --dont_invert
>
> Any help or insight into this would be greatly appreciated!
>
> Many thanks,
>
> Dr Katie Downes
>
